description_long: This is a medium-sized wooden bucket constructed from vertical planks of light-colored wood, featuring visible wood grain and knots throughout its surface. The bucket has a slightly tapered cylindrical shape, wider at the top and narrowing towards the base. Two thick, upright wooden posts extend above the rim on opposite sides, each with a hole through which a sturdy white rope handle is threaded and knotted, allowing for easy carrying. The interior is smooth and matches the exterior wood finish, with no visible lining or coating. There are no visible metal bands or reinforcements, and the bucket appears to be crafted for general utility, possibly for carrying water or other materials in an urban or garden setting.
description: A light wood bucket with a thick white rope handle, vertical plank construction, and visible wood grain and knots.
description_view_0: Front view showing the rope handle draped forward and the prominent wood grain on the central plank.
description_view_1: Side view with both upright posts and rope knots visible, highlighting the bucket's round shape and smooth wood surface.
description_view_2: Rear view with the rope handle pulled to the opposite side, showing a different wood grain pattern and the bucket's symmetry.
description_view_3: Angled view displaying both rope knots and the full arc of the handle, emphasizing the bucket's open top and craftsmanship.
